channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI launches cross-country search to build data center sites for the Stargate project
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden
item.description: Some regulations impose compliance or administrative costs/burdens that have no relation to the work to be performed under a government contract. The post Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bridgestone mejora las capacidades de desarrollo virtual de neumáticos con su propio simulador
item.description: Bridgestone, la compañía especializada en neumáticos y soluciones de movilidad sostenible, ha avanzado la inversión de un nuevo simulador de conducción en circuito (DiL, por sus siglas en inglés) en su centro de I+D en la región de EMEA.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Iberdrola y AWS estrechan lazos: de la compra de energía verde a la nube
item.description: Iberdrola ha ampliado el acuerdo de colaboración que mantenía con el gigante tecnológico Amazon Web Services (AWS) haciendo hincapié en el mercado de Iberia.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The hidden threat of neglected cloud infrastructure
item.description: In 2012, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) issued a public advisory regarding software to monitor and control building systems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: This startup can measure custom insoles with just an iPhone camera
item.description: The Brannock device—that sliding metal gadget used in shoe stores to measure the dimensions of your feet—was invented 100 years ago this year. But footwear fitting hasn’t really gotten more advanced since, says Dan Cataldi, founder and CEO of custom insole maker Groov.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: House lawmakers push to ban AI app DeepSeek from US government devices
item.description: Lawmakers in the House are proposing to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from U.S. government devices.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Deepgram: Revolutionizing Voice AI for Businesses and Developers
item.description: Discover how Deepgram's voice AI platform is transforming the way businesses and developers build speech-to-text, text-to-speech, and full speech-to-speech tools.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: La falta de formación no merma la concepción estratégica de la IA
item.description: Con el punto y final de un 2024 marcado por la irrupción popular de la inteligencia artificial (IA), se podría decir que este nuevo año supondrá la consagración de la tecnología en el plano político, empresarial y social.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The swarm of earthquakes shaking Santorini prompts Greece to declare an emergency
item.description: Greece has declared a state of emergency on Santorini because of the hundreds of undersea earthquakes that have shaken the resort island for nearly a week.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts
item.description: A former White House official who led cyber and tech recruiting efforts said the hiring freeze "will set us back months if not years." The post Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Smart windows take a page from nature's pinecone playbook
item.description: An innovative new window system keeps your home comfortable without using a single watt of electricity and could change the way we think about energy efficiency in buildings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Legislators demand truth about OPM email server
item.description: Two members of the US House of Representatives want answers from the US Office of Personnel Management (OPM) over allegations that a “server of unknown nature” was used last month to “access sensitive government data without regard for crucial security and privacy protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says DOGE will make 'rapid safety upgrades' to air traffic control system
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk on Wednesday said the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) will make "rapid safety upgrades" to the air traffic control systems with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Digma Preemptive Observability Analysis engine tackles AI code bugs
item.description: Digma has unveiled Preemptive Observability Analysis, an observability engine designed to reduce bugs introduced by AI code generation and other coding issues. The commercial version, introduced February 5, follows a limited-capability free version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Warren finds common ground with Trump over debanking concerns
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) seems to have found common ground with President Trump and Republican lawmakers over “debanking” concerns, urging the president Tuesday to take action to prevent Americans from being improperly locked out of the financial system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI chatbot war breaks out with DeepSeek debut, and the winner is…you
item.description: Less than a week after Chinese start-up DeepSeek rocked the tech industry, leading generative AI (genAI) model providers are scrambling to shore up their market foothold and find ways to emulate the newcomer’s tech efficiencies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hands-on with the Windows answer to Apple’s Vision Pro
item.description: Hey, remember the Apple Vision Pro? For a while there, everyone was talking about Apple’s $3,499 headset being the future of productivity. You’d strap a headset to your face and work on large monitors floating in a “mixed reality” world.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AWS tightens default security on Redshift
item.description: Amazon’s security improvements for its AWS Redshift managed data warehouse service are welcome additions, says an expert.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rust update fixes ‘forever’ compilation
item.description: The Rust Team has published a point release update, Rust 1.84.1, to address a few regressions in last month’s Rust 1.84 release, including fixing painfully slow compilation related to the next-generation trait solver.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success
item.description: Louis Koplin, the acting program executive officer for the Navy’s PEO-Digital, said priorities for 2025 include cyber resiliency and expanding BYOD. The post Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
